rulings.Case Title: LAURA HERMOSILLO VS. STEPHEN WILLIAMS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Declaration Of Daniel Berko In Support Declaration Of Laura Hermosillo In Support Separate Statement Of Undisputed Facts In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday April 6, 2018, line 3. PLAINTIFF LAURA HERMOSILLO MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Declaration Of Daniel Berko In Support Declaration Of Laura Hermosillo In Support Separate Statement Of Undisputed Facts In Support GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to issues 3,4, and 5. No opposition filed (See opposition filed by Cross-Complainant). Motion is DENIED as to issue 6 for failure to shift burned. No argument, fact or authority provided as to the applicability of CCC 1697 "Oral Contract." Motion is DENIED as to issue 1, triable issue of fact regarding "confidential communication" and knowledge of recording inside the office. Prevailing party to prepare order in accordance with CCP 437c(g) = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LAURA HERMOSILLO VS. STEPHEN WILLIAMS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 6, 2018, Line 3. CROSS COMPLAINANT STEPHEN WILLIAMS, ESQ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. DENIED. Multiple triable issues of fact as to each issue, including but not limited to 3, 4, 5, 8, 9, 21, 25, 29, 30, 31, 36, 37, etc. Additionally moving party failed to shift its burned on issue 7. Prevailing party to prepare order in accordance with CCP 437c(g). =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICE DARK ET AL VS. PAUL THIBEAULT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday April 6, 2018, line 4. DEFENDANT PAUL THIBEAULT DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts showing that common counts are not barred by the statute of limitations. Demurer is sustained with leave to amend as to all other causes of action to allege in clear and concise language each element of each cause of action against the executor including basis for liability. The causes of action shall only be alleged against the executor in both the caption of the cause of action and in the body of it. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TRINITY MANAGEMENT SERVICES VS. ANN GLOBAL ENTERPRISE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ann Global Enterprise, Inc.'S Notice Of §473(B) Motion And §473(B) Motion To Vacate Entry Of Default And Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday April 6, 2018, line 7. DEFENDANT ANN GLOBAL ENTERPRISE, INC. Defendant Ann Global Enterprise, Inc.'S Notice Of §473(B) Motion And §473(B) Motion To Vacate Entry Of Default And Judgment Hearing Required to address Defendant's position regarding tenancy: while the defendant asserts in the moving papers (but not in the declaration) that "none of Ann's officers and/or agents ever occupied the subject premises or claimed tenancy rights" (Motion 2: 9-11) the proposed answer asserts affirmative defenses that are available to tenants only and asserts that Defendant continued to reside in the unit despite Plaintiff's illegal demands that all defendants move out. (Proposed Answer, paragraph 3e). = (501/REQ)
